articles.title: Weather Today [01 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Snow & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian

articles.full_text:
Weather Today, 01 May 2026: May Day weather across India is turning sharply unstable, with overlapping systems driving heat, rain and thunderstorms at the same time. From northern plains to southern coastlines, the atmosphere is active, making this one of the most mixed pre-monsoon phases of the season.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
Hill regions of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh are expected to see light snowfall only at higher altitudes. Lower valleys will mostly receive rain.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
Rapid heating is driven by dry northwesterly winds and weak early monsoon signals. Clear skies during the day trap more solar radiation, pushing temperatures higher than seasonal averages by 2°C to 4°C in central India.
Which States Get Hit Hardest on May Day?
Check City-Wise Weather Today Update
Delhi-NCR Weather Today
Minimum: 16°C | Maximum: 28°C
Delhi-NCR remains partly cloudy with hot and dry conditions dominating the region and temperatures are rising steadily due to warm winds.

Chennai stays humid with coastal breeze and occasional light rain in isolated pockets during evening hours.

Mumbai sees cloudy skies with mild sea-breeze and chances of light drizzle in some suburbs and hmidity remains high.

Hilly regions see rain in valleys and light snowfall at higher elevations, keeping temperatures much cooler than plains.

Minimum: 16°C | Maximum: 28°C
Punjab experiences partly cloudy skies with dusty winds and isolated light showers in some northern pockets.

Minimum: 16°C | Maximum: 28°C
Ahmedabad stays hot and dry with strong daytime heating and dusty conditions affecting visibility in the afternoon.
City-Wise Sunset & Sunrise Timing
Full City-Wise Rain Forecast Across Major Cities
How Will Rising Temperatures Impact?
Heat stress will rise in central and western India, increasing dehydration risk. Power demand is also expected to spike due to higher cooling needs.
Delhi NCR Weekly Weather Forecast
Delhi’s Air Quality Today – May 01, 2026
AQI: 210 (Moderate to Poor range)
Dust activity is pushing particulate levels higher in afternoon hours.
Major Cities Air Quality Across India
AQI Category Guide
FAQ’s: All About Today’s Weather
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings today?
Yes. Thunderstorms in eastern states and dust storms in northern India may disrupt road and air travel while avoid open areas during lightning activity.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Yes. Western and central India, especially Rajasthan, Gujarat and Madhya Pradesh, are expected to see temperatures climbing above 40°C in several areas.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Yes. Light to moderate rain is likely in eastern India, including Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal and parts of the Northeast, along with scattered showers in southern states.
4. Where will it snow today?
Snowfall is possible only in higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir and parts of Himachal Pradesh. Lower valleys will mostly see rain instead.
5. Is Delhi expected to get heavy rain today?
No. Delhi-NCR will remain mostly dry with partly cloudy skies however, dust winds and a fresh weather system may bring rain again in the coming days.

articles.title: Udhampur poultry farms implement heatwave precautions to protect birds - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
As a heatwave affects parts of Jammu and Kashmir, the Animal Husbandry Department in Udhampur district has introduced preventive measures to protect poultry at the Government Hatchery and Poultry Farm in Kallar. Officials reported on Saturday that efforts are focused on mitigating the impact of increasing temperatures on birds by maintaining suitable environmental conditions and nutrition.
Assistant Manager of Poultry in Udhampur, Dr Surbhi Bharti, outlined important precautions for poultry farmers to follow amid the heatwave. “We should provide proper ventilation to birds in this heat wave. Keep the windows open. Provide fans from both sides so the heat escapes outside. Provide the birds with cold water. Feed the birds either early morning or late evening. Provide them a proper balance of vitamins and minerals,” she said.
The department stressed that these measures are vital to prevent heat stress, which can negatively affect the health and productivity of poultry.
Meanwhile, the Government Veterinary Hospital in the Kallar area marked World Veterinary Day with a programme honouring veterinary professionals and reaffirming their commitment to animal welfare. Participants paid tribute to the historical contributions of the veterinary community and pledged to provide dedicated care for animals.
District Veterinary Hospital Superintendent Dr Anwar Hussain Nagoo emphasised the significance of this year’s theme while addressing the audience. “The World Veterinary Association has selected the theme ‘Veterinarians: Guardians of Food and Health’ for this year. We had called many doctors and experts here… We felicitate the distinguished veterinarians today…” he said.
The event included several doctors and experts who gathered to recognise the role of veterinarians in protecting both animal and public health.
World Veterinary Day is observed annually on April 25 to acknowledge the essential role of veterinarians in animal welfare, food safety, and public health worldwide. This year’s theme highlighted the critical role of vets in the “One Health” approach, particularly in relation to zoonotic diseases, climate change, and food security.
